title: Theoretical Probability
subtitle: Favorable outcomes, total outcomes, and simple probability
intro: Here you'll compute probabilities by reasoning, not by running trials. For equally likely outcomes, the theoretical probability of an event is the number of favorable outcomes divided by the total number of possible outcomes.
You'll practice this with dice and spinners, find the probability of an event NOT happening using complements (the two add up to 1), and compare two events to decide which is more likely.
